Scenario.title: Operation MidnightEclipse Energy Sector Cyber Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Energy Complex Operation MidnightEclipse targets the energy sector with a sophisticated cyber attack plan involving multiple stages and techniques.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Device theft, Weak passwords, Improper configuration, Vulnerability, Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Obsolete products, Injection flaws, Custom APIs, Autonomous system numbers(ASNs), SSL Certificates and Attribution, WHOIS records, contacts and history, TCP/IP ports, Public and private cloud, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Malicious insiders, Social engineering, Insufficient physical security controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: External/Removable media, Loss or Theft of equipment, Attrition, Web, Account takeover, Inadequate encryption, Browser-based attack, Application compromise, Open port, Vulnerability exploit, Ransomware, Malware, Man in The Middle(MiTM), Session hijacking, Brute-force attack, Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS), Watering-hole, Compromised and weak credentials, SIM Swap, Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities, Web forms, IP address and IP blocks, Domains and subdomains, Botnet, Credential compromise, Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS), Replay Attack, Cryptanalysis Attacks, Side Channel Attacks, Hardware Trojans, Covert Attacks, Sleep Deprivation Attack, Clock Skewing, Attacks Targeting System Reputation, Data and Command Integrity Attack, Eavesdropping, Command and Control Attacks, Phishing, Impersonations/Spoofing, Improper usage, Dumpster diving, Advanced Persistent Threat(APT), Baiting, Shoulder surfing, Reverse social engineering, Business Email Compromise(BEC), Advanced Fee, Extortion

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the target's network architecture, system configurations, and potential vulnerabilities through open-source intelligence.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers create a custom tool to exploit the zero-day vulnerability CVE-2024-3400 in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers use the custom tool to exploit the zero-day vulnerability CVE-2024-3400 in the GlobalProtect feature of Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers execute the exploit code on the compromised device to gain control.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers modify the system configuration to maintain access and evade detection.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers gain elevated privileges to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers evade detection by creating duplicate processes to mask malicious activity.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers steal credentials from compromised accounts to move laterally.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan the network for vulnerable devices and services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers move laterally using stolen credentials.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate collected data through alternative ports.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt energy operations by causing a Denial of Service (DoS).

Scenario.summary: Operation MidnightEclipse is a complex cyber attack scenario targeting the energy sector. The attackers employ various techniques, including exploitation of public-facing applications, command and scripting interpreter, and data exfiltration via alternative ports. They also engage in lateral movement, credential access, and defense evasion to achieve their objectives.
